title: Trivia — Present Simple + Frequency
subtitle: 10 multiple-choice questions on Present Simple grammar (Track B-C · Intermediate-Extended)

items.explanation: In Present Simple 3rd person singular (he/she/it) we add -s: "plays". The others are incorrect grammar.

items.text: Choose the correct frequency adverb position:
items.explanation: Frequency adverbs go BEFORE the main verb ("go"). Correct: "I usually go to school."
"Weekdays" = Monday through Friday (the working/school days). "Weekend" = Saturday + Sunday.

items.text: Which is NOT a frequency adverb?
items.explanation: "Yesterday" is a time marker for Past Simple, not a frequency adverb. Frequency adverbs describe HOW OFTEN something happens.

items.text: Complete: "My brother ___ to school by bus."
items.explanation: 3rd person singular needs -s: "My brother GOES to school". "Go" is for I/you/we/they.
Question word + auxiliary + subject + main verb. With "you" we use "do". Correct: "Where do you live?"

items.text: Choose the most appropriate frequency adverb: "I ___ eat fish — I don't like it."
items.explanation: If you don't like something and don't eat it, "never" (0% of the time) is the correct adverb.

items.text: Which sentence uses Present Simple correctly?
options: She is watch TV every evening.
items.explanation: 3rd person: "watches" (verb + -es because watch ends in -ch). "Every evening" is a Present Simple time marker.
In British English: "at the weekend" = on Saturday and Sunday. In American English: "on the weekend". Both are correct.

items.text: Choose the correct chunk for "morning meal":
items.explanation: Breakfast = morning meal (break-fast = breaking the night's fast). Lunch = midday. Dinner = evening. Snack = small bite anytime.
